claims.claim_text: ASIEP treats self-improvement as an evidence object rather than a new self-improvement algorithm.
limitations: The profile covers self-improvement evidence events, not all agent behavior.
reviewer_risks: This may look like a schema-only contribution without the toolchain context.

claims.claim_text: ASIEP uses reference plus digest plus access-policy design instead of embedding raw traces by default.
limitations: Local digest checks do not replace signature verification or access-control enforcement.
reviewer_risks: The privacy story can be overstated if described as full protection.

claims.claim_text: The repository implements validator, repairer, resolver, importer, packager, and evaluator components.
limitations: The components run over local examples and fixtures only.
reviewer_risks: Implementation breadth may be mistaken for mature external integration.

claims.claim_text: profile.json is the agent-readable entrypoint for discovering ASIEP v0.1 capabilities.
reviewer_risks: Agent-readability is structural, not a benchmark of agent performance.

claims.claim_text: The M6 evaluation reports eight metrics.
limitations: Metrics are computed over the local repository corpus.
reviewer_risks: Metric values can look broader than the local corpus supports.

claims.claim_text: Local attack corpus detection recall is 1.0 in the M6 report.
limitations: The attack corpus is small and synthetic.
reviewer_risks: The result must not be framed as open-world adversarial robustness.

claims.claim_text: False positive rate on known valid local fixtures is 0.0 in the M6 report.
limitations: Known valid fixtures are not a broad benchmark set.
reviewer_risks: The metric can be overread as general false-positive behavior.

claims.claim_text: Cross-standard coverage is reported as a local minimal mapping, not external validation.
limitations: Coverage is matrix coverage, not ecosystem conformance testing.
reviewer_risks: Crosswalk language can be mistaken for formal conformance.

claims.claim_text: ASIEP maps to PROV, OTel-like, LangSmith-like, FDO-like, and RO-Crate-like structures with explicit lossiness.
reviewer_risks: Mapping breadth can be read as claiming full interoperability.

claims.claim_text: The current implementation is local-fixture only.
limitations: Future work must test real trace exports.
reviewer_risks: The paper should not hide the local-only scope.

claims.claim_text: Current privacy compliance uses sentinel and key scanning, not full DLP.
limitations: A production privacy review would require dedicated DLP and policy enforcement.

claims.claim_text: Current FDO and RO-Crate outputs are local-like packages, not external certification.
limitations: External registry and community validation are future work.
reviewer_risks: Package language must avoid implying registry submission.

claims.claim_text: ASIEP is complementary to AgentDevel-style release engineering rather than a replacement.
limitations: M8 must verify and cite external related work accurately.
reviewer_risks: Positioning may be weak until external citations are hardened.
